##NAMES=Rietveldite
##RRUFFID=R250086
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Fe(UO_2_)(SO_4_)_2_(H_2_O)_5_
##LOCALITY=Giveaway-Simplot mine, Utah, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Brownish yellow thin blades found on asphaltum in association with ferricopiapite, gypsum, römerite and shumwayite and on pyrite-impregnated sandstone in association with gypsum, halotrichite and römerite. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2017)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250086
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Fe_0.79_Zn_0.08_Mg_0.02_Mn_0.01_)(UO_2_)_0.99_(SO_4_)_2.01_·5.10H_2_O ; Calculated on the basis of 15 O apfu + 0.24 H for charge balance
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.9577 b: 8.3183 c: 11.2971 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1217.67 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
